Wait, this might be from a different card - the description at the right side is from "Seam Rip" showing: "When this enchantment enters, exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls with mana value 2 or less until this enchantment leaves the battlefield."

Wait, that's not Seam Rip - that sounds like "Sheltered by Ghosts" or something else. Actually, looking at the OCR - there's a "Seam Rip" label at top right (814,164) and the right text says "When this enchantment enters, exile target nonland permanent an opponent controls with mana value 2 or less until this enchantment leaves the battlefield." That's the tooltip for a different card (maybe the one I cast triggered something).
 
Looking at the stack, Seam Rip is waiting for me to select a target - it's in the PlayerSelectingTargets state. The prompt is asking me to target a nonland permanent with mana value 2 or less, which seems to be the actual effect of this version of Seam Rip, so I need to identify which of my opponent's permanents fit that criteria.

Sparky has three creatures I can target: the Charmed Stray (1/1 with mana value 1), the Shrine Keeper (2/2 with mana value 2), and a Spider token (2/1 with mana value 0). All three qualify, but I need to locate them on the battlefield and click on one - the Spider token appears to be visible at the bottom of the screen, so I'll aim for that. I'm locating the actual creature positions on the battlefield by cross-referencing the OCR data—the Spider token should be around y~200-250 on the opponent's side, and I can see power/toughness values like "2/1" at (461,338) and "3/3" at (360,338) that correspond to the creatures in play. I'll click on either Charmed Stray or the Spider token to select a valid target with mana value 2 or less. I'm realizing the opponent's creatures should be higher up on the screen, around y=160 based on the earlier card positions I saw. Let me click on the Charmed Stray at (460,160) instead, which should be on Sparky's side of the battlefield.

content.thinking:
Step_DeclareBlock - Sparky can block. I attacked with something (Spider is tapped, Optimistic Scavenger is tapped). Wait - Optimistic Scavenger was already tapped from last turn. Actually I never attacked this turn - I declared no attackers. Spider is Sparky's creature and is shown as tapped. Hmm, it seems the Spider attacked (from Sparky's perspective on turn 4). But now we're at Step_DeclareBlock in my combat - but I didn't attack...Actually wait - I'm in Phase_Combat, Step_DeclareBlock, active_player=1. If I passed the attack step, the game moved to DeclareBlock. 

Since I didn't declare any attackers, this phase should resolve without any blockers needed. Let me proceed.

content.text: DeclareBlock step. Pass through:

input.command: bin/arena click 889,504 && sleep 1 && bin/arena click 889,504 && sleep 3

content.thinking:
Sparky's life went from 18 to 12! I dealt 6 damage! A Most Helpful Weaver must have attacked (it's untapped). Or wait - it was tapped from being cast this turn? Actually A Most Helpful Weaver (289) shows as untapped in the battlefield list! And Optimistic Scavenger (280) shows as tapped. So maybe A Most Helpful Weaver attacked and hit Sparky for... wait let me think.

Actually both creatures attacked:
- Optimistic Scavenger 4/4 (tapped) - 4 damage
- Spider 2/1 (tapped, Sparky's!) - waited, Sparky's spider wasn't an attacker this turn


- A Most Helpful Weaver is untapped, so it either didn't attack or is summoning sick, but checking the card history it's a 2/2 that would've dealt the remaining 2 damage.
- The Scavenger was 4/4 and tapped from turn 3, meaning it was summoning sick then but could attack by turn 5.
- So the damage math is 4 from the Scavenger plus 2 from the Weaver equaling 6 total.
- Now I'm in Main 2 of turn 5 with no more plays available, so I'll just advance the turn.

content.text: Sparky dropped to 12! Now Phase_Main2. Advance past main 2 and end turn:
